**Timothy Samuel Elliott**[[9]](#cite_note-9) (born December 24, 1986) is an American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who competes in the [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC). Competing professionally since 2009, Elliott is the former [Titan FC](/wiki/Titan_FC "Titan FC") [Flyweight Champion](/wiki/Titan_Fighting_Championships#Flyweight_Championship "Titan Fighting Championships") and the winner of [The Ultimate Fighter: Tournament of Champions](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Tournament_of_Champions "The Ultimate Fighter: Tournament of Champions"). As of March 3, 2026, he is #11 in the [UFC flyweight rankings](/wiki/List_of_current_UFC_fighters#Rankings "List of current UFC fighters").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Elliott was born on December 24, 1986, in [Arkansas City, Kansas](/wiki/Arkansas_City,_Kansas "Arkansas City, Kansas").[[1]](#cite_note-tsp23-1) When he was in fourth grade, Elliott moved with his father to [Haysville, Kansas](/wiki/Haysville,_Kansas "Haysville, Kansas"), where he started [wrestling](/wiki/Scholastic_wrestling "Scholastic wrestling").[[1]](#cite_note-tsp23-1) Elliott originally attended [Campus High School](/wiki/Campus_High_School "Campus High School") where he was a state wrestling runner-up, but transferred to [Wichita South High School](/wiki/Wichita_South_High_School "Wichita South High School") during his junior year. Elliott then became a state champion at Wichita South his senior year in 2005.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

He was on the [wrestling](/wiki/Collegiate_wrestling "Collegiate wrestling") team at [Labette Community College](/wiki/Labette_Community_College "Labette Community College"), before transferring to the [University of Central Oklahoma](/wiki/University_of_Central_Oklahoma "University of Central Oklahoma"), earning All-American honors, placing every year during his collegiate wrestling career and becoming the [NJCAA](/wiki/NJCAA "NJCAA") wrestling national champion in 2007.[[1]](#cite_note-tsp23-1)[[12]](#cite_note-12) He earned a bachelor's degree in Science and General Studies.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Elliott compiled a record of 8–2–1 in his first 11 fights before signing with the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), including a notable [KO](/wiki/KO "KO") victory over former [UFC Lightweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_UFC_champions#Lightweight_Championship "List of UFC champions") [Jens Pulver](/wiki/Jens_Pulver "Jens Pulver").

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Elliott signed with UFC on April 17, 2012.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In his UFC debut, Elliott faced [The Ultimate Fighter 14](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller") winner [John Dodson](/wiki/John_Dodson_(fighter) "John Dodson (fighter)") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3"), replacing an injured [Darren Uyenoyama](/wiki/Darren_Uyenoyama "Darren Uyenoyama").[[14]](#cite_note-14) Elliott lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In his second fight, Elliott faced Jared Papazian on December 15, 2012, at [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Carwin vs. Team Nelson Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Carwin_vs._Team_Nelson_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Carwin vs. Team Nelson Finale").[[16]](#cite_note-16) He won the fight via unanimous decision (30–25, 30–25, 30–26). The fight also earned him the *Fight of the Night* bonus.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-tuf16b-18)

For his third fight, Elliott faced [Louis Gaudinot](/wiki/Louis_Gaudinot "Louis Gaudinot") on August 31, 2013, at [UFC 164](/wiki/UFC_164 "UFC 164").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He won the fight via unanimous decision.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Elliott fought [Ali Bagautinov](/wiki/Ali_Bagautinov "Ali Bagautinov") at [UFC 167](/wiki/UFC_167 "UFC 167") on November 16, 2013.[[21]](#cite_note-21) The bout marked his first appearance on the main card of a pay-per-view event in the fight. He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Elliott faced [Joseph Benavidez](/wiki/Joseph_Benavidez "Joseph Benavidez") on April 26, 2014, at [UFC 172](/wiki/UFC_172 "UFC 172").[[23]](#cite_note-23) He lost the fight via submission in the first round.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Elliott was expected to face [Wilson Reis](/wiki/Wilson_Reis "Wilson Reis") on August 23, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 49](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_49 "UFC Fight Night 49").[[25]](#cite_note-25) However, Elliott pulled out of the bout in the days leading up to the event.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Elliott faced [Zach Makovsky](/wiki/Zach_Makovsky "Zach Makovsky") on February 15, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 60](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_60 "UFC Fight Night 60").[[27]](#cite_note-27) He lost the fight by unanimous decision. Following the fight, he was released from the organization.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

### Titan FC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Titan FC")]

Elliott made his debut and faced former UFC Flyweight [Iliarde Santos](/wiki/Iliarde_Santos "Iliarde Santos") at [Titan FC 34](/wiki/List_of_Titan_FC_events#Titan_FC_34 "List of Titan FC events") for the inaugural [Titan FC Flyweight Championship](/wiki/Titan_Fighting_Championships#Flyweight_Championship "Titan Fighting Championships").[[29]](#cite_note-29) He won the fight by a dominant unanimous decision and picked up the Titan FC Flyweight Championship.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

For his first title defense, Elliott faced undefeated prospect and promotional newcomer Felipe Efrain at [Titan FC 35](/wiki/List_of_Titan_FC_events#Titan_FC_35 "List of Titan FC events") on September 19, 2015.[[31]](#cite_note-31) He won the fight by submission in the second round.

Elliott next faced former UFC Bantamweight Pedro Nobre at [Titan FC 37](/wiki/List_of_Titan_FC_events#Titan_FC_37 "List of Titan FC events") on March 5, 2016. He won the fight by unanimous decision for his second successful title defense.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

On July 21, 2016, Elliott was announced as a cast member on the [24th season](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Benavidez_vs._Team_Cejudo "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Benavidez vs. Team Cejudo") of [The Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter"). The winner of the show, won a chance to fight [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson_(fighter) "Demetrious Johnson (fighter)") for the UFC Flyweight Championship.[[33]](#cite_note-33) Despite being ranked as the number 3 seed, Elliott was selected as the first overall pick by former opponent [Joseph Benavidez](/wiki/Joseph_Benavidez "Joseph Benavidez").[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In his first fight, Elliott faced 14th ranked 'Team Cejudo' fighter Charlie Alaniz. He submitted Aniz in the first round by bulldog choke.[[35]](#cite_note-35) In the quarterfinals, Elliott faced 6th ranked 'Team Cejudo' fighter and former [Caged](/wiki/Caged_(TV_series) "Caged (TV series)") contestant [Matt Schnell](/wiki/Matt_Schnell "Matt Schnell"). Despite being struck with a devastating groin kick in the opening moments of the bout, he was able to recover and submit Schnell in the first round by front choke.[[36]](#cite_note-36) In the semifinals, Elliott faced 15th ranked 'Team Benavidez' teammate [Eric Shelton](/wiki/Eric_Shelton_(fighter) "Eric Shelton (fighter)"). After two competitive rounds, Elliott was declared the winner by majority decision.[[37]](#cite_note-37) Elliott fought 5th ranked 'Team Benavidez' teammate [Hiromasa Ougikubo](/wiki/Hiromasa_Ougikubo "Hiromasa Ougikubo") in the final round. Elliott won the fight by unanimous decision 30–27 to win the tournament.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

### Return to the UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Return to the UFC")]

As a result of winning [The Ultimate Fighter 24](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Tournament_of_Champions "The Ultimate Fighter: Tournament of Champions"), Elliott won the opportunity to fight flyweight champion [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson "Demetrious Johnson"). He fought Johnson on December 3, 2016, at [The Ultimate Fighter 24 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Tournament_of_Champions#The_Ultimate_Fighter_24_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Tournament of Champions") for the flyweight championship.[[39]](#cite_note-39)
Elliott lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

Elliott faced [Louis Smolka](/wiki/Louis_Smolka "Louis Smolka") on April 15, 2017, at [UFC on Fox 24](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_24 "UFC on Fox 24").[[41]](#cite_note-41) He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[42]](#cite_note-42) The win earned Elliott his second *Fight of the Night* bonus award, and his first since returning the organization.[[43]](#cite_note-fox24b-43)

Elliott faced [Ben Nguyen](/wiki/Ben_Nguyen "Ben Nguyen") on June 10, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 110](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_110 "UFC Fight Night 110").[[44]](#cite_note-44) He was submitted with a rear-naked choke in the opening minute of the first round.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

Elliott was expected to face [Justin Scoggins](/wiki/Justin_Scoggins "Justin Scoggins") on December 16, 2017, at [UFC on Fox 26](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_26 "UFC on Fox 26").[[46]](#cite_note-46) However, Scoggins pulled out of the bout in early December citing a back injury and was replaced by Pietro Menga.[[47]](#cite_note-47) The bout was cancelled as Menga failed to show to the weigh in because he did not make weight.[[48]](#cite_note-48) Elliott was offered the fight at catchweight at 131 pounds, which would have given him 10% of Menga's fight purse, but he declined.[[49]](#cite_note-Elliott_Menga-49)

After the fight against Menga was cancelled, the UFC offered Elliott a fight at [UFC 219](/wiki/UFC_219 "UFC 219"), which Elliott only accepted under the condition he fights somebody who has never missed weight and has never backed out of a fight at the last minute.[[50]](#cite_note-50) Elliott fought promotional newcomer [Mark De La Rosa](/wiki/Mark_De_La_Rosa "Mark De La Rosa") at bantamweight.[[51]](#cite_note-51) He won in the second round by submission via an anaconda choke. After the fight, he dedicated the win to his coach, Robert Follis, who died earlier in the month.[[52]](#cite_note-52) For the fight, Elliott won a $50,000 [Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") bonus, his first performance of the night bonus and third fight bonus in the UFC overall.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

Elliott tore his [ACL](/wiki/Anterior_cruciate_ligament_injury "Anterior cruciate ligament injury") in June 2018. He underwent successful surgery on it on June 14. He stated at the time that he expected miss at least a year before he could fight again.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

Elliott faced [Deiveson Figueiredo](/wiki/Deiveson_Figueiredo "Deiveson Figueiredo") on October 12, 2019, at [UFC on ESPN+ 19](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN%2B_19 "UFC on ESPN+ 19").[[55]](#cite_note-55) He lost the fight via submission in the first round.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

Elliott faced [Askar Askarov](/wiki/Askar_Askarov "Askar Askarov") January 18, 2020 at [UFC 246](/wiki/UFC_246 "UFC 246").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

Elliot faced promotional newcomer [Brandon Royval](/wiki/Brandon_Royval "Brandon Royval") on May 30, 2020, at [UFC on ESPN 9](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_9 "UFC on ESPN 9").[[59]](#cite_note-59) He lost the fight via submission in the second round.[[60]](#cite_note-60) This fight earned Elliott his third [*Fight of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") award.[[61]](#cite_note-UFConESPN9-61) Elliott accepted a fight against [Tyson Nam](/wiki/Tyson_Nam "Tyson Nam") at [UFC on ESPN 10](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_10 "UFC on ESPN 10"), but the NSAC refused to allow him to fight since they placed him on a medical suspension after his previous fight.[[62]](#cite_note-62)

Elliott fought [Ryan Benoit](/wiki/Ryan_Benoit "Ryan Benoit") on July 16, 2020, at [UFC on ESPN 13](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_13 "UFC on ESPN 13").[[63]](#cite_note-63) He won the fight by unanimous decision, securing his first win since 2017. Prior to the fight, he had signed a new four-fight contract with the promotion.[[64]](#cite_note-64) On August 3, 2020, it was reported that Elliott had tested positive for marijuana after the fight and is facing a suspension.[[65]](#cite_note-65) Later that day, he posted to his [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") blaming the positive test on taking the fight on short notice.[[66]](#cite_note-66) Elliott was suspended four and a half months and fined 15% if his purse by the [Nevada State Athletic Commission](/wiki/Nevada_State_Athletic_Commission "Nevada State Athletic Commission") on September 3, 2020, after positive for [marijuana](/wiki/Marijuana "Marijuana"). The suspension is retroactive to June 17, 2020, and he will be eligible to compete again on November 2, 2020.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

Elliott was expected to face [Jordan Espinosa](/wiki/Jordan_Espinosa "Jordan Espinosa") on January 16, 2021, at [UFC on ABC 1](/wiki/UFC_on_ABC_1 "UFC on ABC 1").[[68]](#cite_note-68) However, Espinosa tested positive for COVID-19 in late December and the pairing was moved to [UFC 259](/wiki/UFC_259 "UFC 259").[[69]](#cite_note-69) Elliott won the fight via unanimous decision.[[70]](#cite_note-70) During the fight, Elliott accused Espinoza of being a "woman beater" after someone sent Elliott screenshot of messages between Espinoza and an unnamed girl along with images of choke marks on a person's neck. Espinoza responded by saying, "you don't know the whole story". Police stated that Espinoza had never been convicted of domestic abuse.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

Elliott was scheduled to face [Su Mudaerji](/wiki/Su_Mudaerji "Su Mudaerji") on June 26, 2021, at [UFC Fight Night 190](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_190 "UFC Fight Night 190")[[72]](#cite_note-72) However, Mudaerji had to pull out off the bout due to a knee injury that required surgery.[[73]](#cite_note-73)

Elliott faced [Matheus Nicolau](/wiki/Matheus_Nicolau "Matheus Nicolau") on October 9, 2021, at [UFC Fight Night 194](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_194 "UFC Fight Night 194").[[74]](#cite_note-74) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

Elliott faced [Tagir Ulanbekov](/wiki/Tagir_Ulanbekov "Tagir Ulanbekov") on March 5, 2022, at [UFC 272](/wiki/UFC_272 "UFC 272").[[76]](#cite_note-76) He won the fight via unanimous decision.[[77]](#cite_note-77) 12 out of 17 media scores gave it to Ulanbekov.[[78]](#cite_note-78)

Elliot was scheduled to face [Amir Albazi](/wiki/Amir_Albazi "Amir Albazi") on June 25, 2022 at [UFC on ESPN 38](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_38 "UFC on ESPN 38")[[79]](#cite_note-79) but pulled out due to undisclosed reasons.[[80]](#cite_note-80)

Elliot was scheduled to face Allan Nascimento on June 3, 2023, at [UFC on ESPN 46](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_46 "UFC on ESPN 46").[[81]](#cite_note-81) However on April 28, Nascimento pulled out due to undisclosed reasons and was replaced by Victor Altamirano.[[82]](#cite_note-NascimentoOut-82) He won the fight via unanimous decision.[[83]](#cite_note-83)

Elliott faced [Muhammad Mokaev](/wiki/Muhammad_Mokaev "Muhammad Mokaev") at [UFC 294](/wiki/UFC_294 "UFC 294") on October 21, 2023.[[84]](#cite_note-84) He lost the fight via arm-triangle choke submission in the third round.[[85]](#cite_note-85)

Elliott faced [Su Mudaerji](/wiki/Su_Mudaerji "Su Mudaerji"), replacing injured Allan Nascimento, on December 9, 2023, at [UFC Fight Night 233](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_233 "UFC Fight Night 233").[[86]](#cite_note-Elliott_in-86) He won the fight via arm-triangle choke in round one.[[87]](#cite_note-87) This fight earned him the *[Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[88]](#cite_note-UFN233-88)

Elliott was scheduled to face [Tatsuro Taira](/wiki/Tatsuro_Taira "Tatsuro Taira") on May 18, 2024 at [UFC Fight Night 241](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_241 "UFC Fight Night 241").[[89]](#cite_note-89) However, due to Elliott's undisclosed injury, the bout was scrapped.[[90]](#cite_note-90)

Elliott faced former [UFC Flyweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Flyweight_Championship "UFC Flyweight Championship") challenger [Kai Asakura](/wiki/Kai_Asakura "Kai Asakura") on August 16, 2025 at [UFC 319](/wiki/UFC_319 "UFC 319").[[91]](#cite_note-91) He won the fight via a guillotine choke in round two.[[92]](#cite_note-92) This win earned Elliott his third [*Performance of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") award.[[93]](#cite_note-UFC319-93)

Elliott is scheduled to face [Steve Erceg](/wiki/Steve_Erceg "Steve Erceg") on May 2, 2026 at [UFC Fight Night 275](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_275 "UFC Fight Night 275").[[94]](#cite_note-94)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tim_Elliott&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Personal life")]

He was in a relationship with fellow UFC fighter [Gina Mazany](/wiki/Gina_Mazany "Gina Mazany").[[95]](#cite_note-95) On July 28, 2020, Elliott announced on his Instagram that he and Mazany were engaged.[[96]](#cite_note-96) Elliott and Mazany have since divorced.[[97]](#cite_note-97) He has one daughter from a previous relationship.[[98]](#cite_note-98)
